data.title: December inflation report shows steady prices with CPI rising 2.7%; Fed holds rates, defends stance against political pressure.
data.description: December's inflation report showed that prices were holding steady, with the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rising 2.7% year-over-year, remaining unchanged from the previous month. However, core services inflation excluding housing, known as "super core," softened to 2.76%. Despite this, some experts, like Jeffrey Roach, Chief Economist for LPL Financial, believe that the monthly rate is still "a bit too hot." Roach suggests that the Federal Reserve may hold off on rate adjustments this month but could potentially consider a rate cut by April due to economic risks leaning towards a weakening labor market. Eric Teal, Chief Investment Officer for Comerica Wealth Management, predicts that inflation will likely stay between 2.2% and 2.7%. Meanwhile, strategist Charlie Bilello defended the Federal Reserve's decision to maintain rates amidst political pressure, labeling outside interference as dangerous "price fixing." The market considers the current inflation level "good enough" but not low enough for an immediate rate cut by the Federal Reserve.

data.title: TITAN signs 10-year deal with EPS for 5 million tons of fly ash, boosting ACMs platform.
data.description: Brussels-based company TITAN Group (TITC) is ramping up its Alternative Cementitious Materials (ACMs) platform through a newly inked 10-year deal with the state-owned power utility Electric Power of Serbia (EPS). This agreement grants TITAN access to around 5 million tons of fresh fly ash from the TENT B “Obrenovac B” power plant. This move bolsters TITAN's strategic resource pool for alternative cementitious materials, propelling its expansion efforts in this sector.

data.title: Dow fell by almost 400 points due to rising inflation, fear & greed index remains in "Greed" zone. Market sentiment declines.
data.description: The most recent data from the CNN Money Fear and Greed index shows a shift towards a less positive market sentiment, although the index is still indicating a state of "Greed". The U.S. stock market experienced a decline on Tuesday, with the Dow Jones index dropping by nearly 400 points throughout the trading session. In December, the Consumer Price Index saw a 2.7% increase compared to the previous year, aligning with both the previous data and predictions made by economists. The core CPI, excluding volatile food and energy prices, showed a 2.6% rise year over year, slightly below what was anticipated. Despite exceeding earnings expectations, JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) saw a decrease of over 4% in its stock value due to concerns over weaker investment-banking fees and cautious guidance provided by the company. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: FMF's 5th Ministerial Roundtable in Riyadh includes 100 governments and 59 multilaterals to shape future of minerals development.
data.description: In Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, on January 14, 2026, the 5th Ministerial Roundtable of the Future Minerals Forum (FMF) wrapped up successfully. This event signifies a crucial step in worldwide collaboration concerning minerals, highlighting the move towards a more accountable and durable approach.

data.title: Luxembourg approves Ripple for EU expansion, complementing its UK authorization. This moves Ripple closer to MiCA compliance.
data.description: A recent development in Luxembourg has given a significant boost to Ripple's expansion efforts in the European Union. With this initial approval, Ripple is furthering its goal of complying with the Markets in Crypto-Assets regulation. This approval comes after Ripple recently received authorization in the UK, highlighting the company's commitment to achieving regulatory compliance in various jurisdictions. This move sets the stage for Ripple's expansion in the EU, showcasing its strategic approach to navigating the regulatory landscape.
